title: Nitrogen and sulfur
summary: 9701 AS nitrogen and sulfur chemistry — Haber process, oxides, acid rain, and environmental impact.
learningObjectives: Describe the Haber process and equilibrium conditions.
Explain formation of NO/NO₂ and acid rain.
Outline the Contact process for sulfuric acid.
State tests for ammonia and sulfur dioxide.

simpleExplanation.title: N₂ and S in industry and the environment
simpleExplanation.summary: N₂ is unreactive due to the triple bond — Haber conditions overcome this. SO₂ from combustion leads to acid rain via SO₃ and H₂SO₄.
steps: Lightning/engines: N₂ + O₂ → NO → NO₂ → nitric acid in rain.
Contact: 2SO₂ + O₂ ⇌ 2SO₃; SO₃ + H₂SO₄ → H₂S₂O₇ then dilute.
NH₃ test: pungent, red litmus blue; SO₂: decolourises K₂MnO₄.

sections.content: **Nitrogen and sulfur** (12.1) connects **equilibrium** (7.1) to **environmental chemistry**. Know **Haber** and **Contact** conditions and **catalytic role** of V₂O₅.

sections.label: Mark a nitrogen and sulfur question
